---
title: "Horned Bladderwort (Utricularia cornuta)"
slug: "utricularia-cornuta"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621806746/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Horned Bladderwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Lentibulariaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Lentibulariaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621806746/medium.jpg" alt="Horned Bladderwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jecmanatee">
jecmanatee</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341553809">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Lentibulariaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=62372)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common/Uncommon species in Florida found in open, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small carnivorous plant characterized by its fine, thread-like submerged leaves and tiny bladder traps used to capture microscopic prey. In Florida, it is found in shallow, acidic wetlands such as bogs, seepage areas, and wet depressions. It typically blooms during the warmer months, producing delicate yellow flowers, and obtains nutrients by trapping small aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/62372-Utricularia-cornuta)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/1269)
